your_book:
Jaleel's book had a quiet Thursday, finishing essentially flat — unrealized loss of $23.23 on a $21,589 account, a rounding error in either direction. The day's standout mover was Danaher, up 2.65% to $1,370.74, followed by Broadcom (+3.2% to $401.11) and Lululemon (+2.54% to $1,048.59). The software bloc — ADBE (+0.77%) and NOW (+0.97%) — got a modest lift from a Guggenheim note pushing back on AI-disruption fears, which is fine as a one-day reprieve but doesn't resolve the underlying narrative pressure on either name. On the other side, RKLB dropped another 0.96% and now sits -16.3% from cost; Abbott slipped 0.86% on what appears to be defensive sector rotation.

The drift worth watching is the ADBE/BSX pairing. ADBE is the book's largest position at 16.2% of the account ($3,488) and carries a -9.2% cost-basis loss despite a STRONG BUY verdict and a QARP of 73.1 — price and thesis are pulling in opposite directions, and the Guggenheim headline doesn't change the CFO exit or the ARR de-emphasis dynamic flagged in the risk register. BSX is similarly adrift: -6.0% in the hole, STRONG BUY rated, yet guidance was slashed to 7–8.5% growth with management guiding flat through Q3. Both positions are rated as if the thesis is intact while the price action disagrees.

The near-term calendar is the real watchlist: ABT reports July 16, NOW reports July 22, and BSX reports July 29. That's three of the top four positions by weight delivering earnings in the next three weeks. RKLB continues to bleed with an AVOID verdict and no near-term catalyst visible in the data — that one deserves a hard look before the tape makes the decision for Jaleel.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -16.3% loss, still 7.3% of book
attention.note: RKLB carries the portfolio's lowest QARP (39.3), an explicit AVOID verdict, and is down another 0.96% today — the data provides no thesis to hold and the position is large enough to matter.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings July 29 — thesis under live pressure
attention.note: FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3 yet BSX still carries a STRONG BUY verdict; earnings in 20 days will either validate the gap or widen it.

attention.headline: Largest position, -9.2% loss, narrative still broken despite today's bounce
attention.note: At 16.2% of the account the concentration alone demands scrutiny, and today's Guggenheim-driven lift doesn't address the CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis, or AI-moat fear that drove the selloff post-beat.

attention.headline: Earnings July 22 — high-multiple leader, macro beta is real
attention.note: NOW is the second-largest weight at 14.1% ($3,047), trades at a premium multiple, and the risk flag explicitly notes it leads drawdowns in any software de-rate; Q2 print in under two weeks is the next binary moment.
